tasks.description: Edit gpu_orchestrator/runpod/worker_startup.template.sh: replace the current `else` branch (lines ~374-379) of the sentinel-skip block with capture-and-branch logic. Keep `rm -f "$SYNC_SENTINEL"` first, then capture `uv sync --locked --python 3.10 --extra cuda124` stdout/stderr to a `$(mktemp)` file under `set +e`, record `SYNC_RC=$?`, restore `set -e`, and append the captured output to $LOG_FILE. Then branch with if/elif/else: (a) on `SYNC_RC -eq 0`, write `printf '%s\n' "$EXPECTED_INPUTS_HASH" > "$SYNC_SENTINEL"`, log the existing-style success line, and `rm -f "$SYNC_OUTPUT"`; (b) elif `grep -qiE 'lockfile.*needs to be updated' "$SYNC_OUTPUT"`, log `⚠️  Lockfile drift detected on origin/main; regenerating lockfile in-pod and re-syncing (visit reigh-worker CI to fix upstream)`, `rm -f "$SYNC_OUTPUT"`, run `"$UV_BIN" lock --python 3.10 >> "$LOG_FILE" 2>&1` and `"$UV_BIN" sync --python 3.10 --extra cuda124 >> "$LOG_FILE" 2>&1` (no `--locked`) — `set -e` is in effect so either failure aborts via the existing ERR trap — then recompute `UV_LOCK_SHA256="$(sha256sum uv.lock | awk '{print $1}')"` and recompute `EXPECTED_INPUTS_HASH` using the same hashing recipe used earlier in the template (uv-version, python: 3.10, extras: cuda124, uv-lock-sha256, pyproject-sha256), then `printf '%s\n' "$EXPECTED_INPUTS_HASH" > "$SYNC_SENTINEL"` and log `✅ uv sync complete via auto-heal; sentinel refreshed (post-heal hash=$EXPECTED_INPUTS_HASH; uv=$UV_VERSION_STR)`; (c) else log `❌ uv sync --locked failed (rc=$SYNC_RC) and output did not match lockfile-drift marker; aborting`, `rm -f "$SYNC_OUTPUT"`, and `exit "$SYNC_RC"`. Do not modify any other part of the template; preserve $PYPROJECT_SHA256/$UV_VERSION_STR variable references already computed earlier in the script.
tasks.executor_notes: Updated worker_startup.template.sh sentinel else branch to capture uv sync --locked output with mktemp under set +e, restore set -e, log captured output, branch on SYNC_RC, auto-heal only on grep -qiE 'lockfile.*needs to be updated', recompute UV_LOCK_SHA256 and EXPECTED_INPUTS_HASH after uv lock/recovery sync, clean up SYNC_OUTPUT in all three arms, and exit with original SYNC_RC for non-drift failures. Verified with bash -n and structural substring/count checks.
commands_run: python structural substring/count check for set +e, SYNC_RC, drift grep, uv lock, unlocked recovery sync, post-heal recompute, warning/success/abort markers, 3 SYNC_OUTPUT removals, and 2 sentinel writes

tasks.description: Add a new test `test_rendered_startup_script_auto_heals_lockfile_drift` to tests/gpu_orchestrator/runpod/test_startup_script.py that renders the template (using the existing helper used by neighboring tests) and asserts the new structure. Required assertions: (1) presence of `set +e`, `SYNC_RC=$?`, and a matching `set -e` near the captured sync; (2) presence of the literal substring `grep -qiE 'lockfile.*needs to be updated'`; (3) presence of `⚠️  Lockfile drift detected`; (4) both `"$UV_BIN" lock --python 3.10` and the unlocked recovery `"$UV_BIN" sync --python 3.10 --extra cuda124` are present; (5) `UV_LOCK_SHA256="$(sha256sum uv.lock | awk '{print $1}')"` appears AFTER the `⚠️  Lockfile drift detected` warning index (i.e. recompute happens inside the auto-heal arm); (6) ordering inside the auto-heal arm: warning_index < `"$UV_BIN" lock --python 3.10` index < recovery-sync index < auto-heal sentinel-write index — use `script.rindex('printf \'%s\\n\' "$EXPECTED_INPUTS_HASH" > "$SYNC_SENTINEL"')` to disambiguate from the success-arm write earlier in the file; (7) presence of the literal substring `✅ uv sync complete via auto-heal`; (8) presence of the non-drift abort substring `output did not match lockfile-drift marker`. Add a brief docstring noting that the existing `test_rendered_startup_script_gates_uv_sync_on_inputs_sentinel` continues to pass because the success arm preserves `rm -f "$SYNC_SENTINEL"`, the literal `"$UV_BIN" sync --locked` substring, and the FIRST occurrence of `printf '%s\n' "$EXPECTED_INPUTS_HASH" > "$SYNC_SENTINEL"` in document order.
tasks.executor_notes: Added test_rendered_startup_script_auto_heals_lockfile_drift using startup_script.render_startup_script. The test asserts set +e / locked sync / SYNC_RC / set -e ordering, the exact drift grep, warning marker, uv lock, unlocked recovery uv sync, post-warning UV_LOCK_SHA256 recompute, auto-heal sentinel write ordering via script.rindex(...), auto-heal success marker, and non-drift abort marker. The existing sentinel-skip test was left unchanged and the full startup-script test file passes. First full-file run failed because the new test looked for set +e after the locked sync; corrected the assertion to the actual intended order set +e < locked sync < SYNC_RC < set -e, then reran the full file successfully.

tasks.description: Run the targeted suite first: `pytest tests/gpu_orchestrator/runpod/test_startup_script.py -x`. Then run the broader suite: `pytest tests/gpu_orchestrator/ -x`. If anything fails, read the error, fix the code (template or test as appropriate), and re-run until both pass — do not create new test files beyond T2's addition. Then write a small throwaway shell script in /tmp that simulates the production failure: create a temp dir, write a fake `uv.lock`+`pyproject.toml`, and inline-extract the rendered `else` block's branching shape (or simply assert via `python -c` that rendering the template and grepping it shows the auto-heal substrings present). Run it to confirm the rendered script behaves as expected on the drift marker, then delete the script. Finally, do a manual visual diff of the rendered template's `else` block to confirm: `set -e` is restored before the auto-heal `uv lock`/recovery `uv sync` (so a real lock-resolution failure aborts via the ERR trap), `$SYNC_OUTPUT` is removed in all three arms (no temp-file leak), and the success arm's sentinel-write line is structurally identical to today (preserving the existing test's `script.index(...)` lookups).
tasks.executor_notes: Verified the completed change. `pytest tests/gpu_orchestrator/runpod/test_startup_script.py -x` passed with 7 tests. `pytest tests/gpu_orchestrator/ -x` passed with 26 tests and 2 dependency warnings. A temporary /tmp verifier rendered the startup script and confirmed the drift grep, warning, uv lock -> unlocked uv sync -> post-heal sentinel ordering, post-heal recompute, non-drift abort marker, and three SYNC_OUTPUT cleanups; the temporary script was deleted afterward. Manual rendered-block review confirmed set -e is restored before the auto-heal uv lock/recovery sync commands, SYNC_OUTPUT is removed in all three arms, and the success-arm sentinel write remains before the auto-heal sentinel write.

watch_items: FLAG-001: the new ordering test must use `script.rindex(...)` (not `index`) for the auto-heal sentinel-write line, because the success arm contains the identical `printf '%s\n' "$EXPECTED_INPUTS_HASH" > "$SYNC_SENTINEL"` literal earlier in the rendered file. Using `index` would resolve to the success-arm position and the ordering check would mis-fire.

Existing test `test_rendered_startup_script_gates_uv_sync_on_inputs_sentinel` uses `script.index(...)` for `rm -f "$SYNC_SENTINEL"` < `"$UV_BIN" sync --locked` < `printf '%s\n' "$EXPECTED_INPUTS_HASH" > "$SYNC_SENTINEL"`. Keep `rm -f "$SYNC_SENTINEL"` BEFORE the captured sync, keep the literal `"$UV_BIN" sync --locked` substring (do not break it across lines or alias it), and keep the success-arm sentinel-write as the FIRST occurrence in document order — otherwise the existing test breaks.

Sentinel correctness: after auto-heal, `EXPECTED_INPUTS_HASH` MUST be recomputed using the regenerated `uv.lock` (recompute `UV_LOCK_SHA256` via `sha256sum uv.lock | awk '{print $1}'`). Reuse the same hashing recipe and field order already used earlier in the template — confirm by reading the script, do not invent a new recipe.

`set -e` is on at the top of the script. Locally toggle `set +e` ONLY around the captured `uv sync --locked` invocation; restore `set -e` before `uv lock` and the recovery `uv sync` so any real lock-resolution failure aborts via the existing ERR trap (do not swallow real errors).

Drift marker is intentionally narrow: `grep -qiE 'lockfile.*needs to be updated'`. Do NOT broaden to generic `lockfile` matching — that would misclassify real failures as drift and silently regenerate the lock.
Recovery `uv sync` MUST omit `--locked`. Recovery `uv lock` keeps `--python 3.10` only (no `--extra`), matching the brief.
Clean up `$SYNC_OUTPUT` (mktemp file) in all three arms — success, auto-heal, non-drift abort — to avoid leaking temp files into pod volumes that may persist across boots.

Bash-3 compatibility: no bashisms beyond what the existing template already uses. No `[[ ... ]]`, no `mapfile`, no associative arrays — stick with `[ ... ]` and POSIX-style constructs that match the surrounding style.

Idempotency assumption: if upstream lockfile is repaired between boots, the freshly-pulled `uv.lock` sha will match the sentinel written from the prior auto-heal run — the fast-path skip resumes. Confirm by reading the sentinel-comparison logic earlier in the template; do not change it.
Do NOT modify `gpu_orchestrator/runpod/startup_script.py` — the template is loaded verbatim and no new placeholders are introduced.
Do NOT add Sentry/Slack alerting; the visible warning log line is sufficient for this iteration.
Verifiability flags (verifiability-0, verifiability-1) on success criteria 8/9 are subjective-judgment items — the executor should record a short reasoning note rather than a code-driven check.

Debt watch (do not worsen): scaling_decision.py:70 legacy duplication and periodic.py:175-229 failsafe duplication, plus the `is_failsafe_stale` derived-state opportunity — none of these are in scope, but avoid editing those files in passing.

sense_checks.question: Does the rendered template's else branch capture `uv sync --locked` rc with `set +e`/`set -e` toggling, branch via if/elif/else with the exact drift marker `grep -qiE 'lockfile.*needs to be updated'`, recompute UV_LOCK_SHA256 + EXPECTED_INPUTS_HASH inside the auto-heal arm before writing the sentinel, run `uv lock --python 3.10` then `uv sync --python 3.10 --extra cuda124` (no `--locked`) under restored `set -e`, clean up `$SYNC_OUTPUT` in all three arms, and on non-drift failure exit with the original `$SYNC_RC`?
sense_checks.executor_note: Yes. The else branch keeps rm -f "$SYNC_SENTINEL" first, captures locked sync output to SYNC_OUTPUT with set +e/SYNC_RC=$?/set -e, checks exactly grep -qiE 'lockfile.*needs to be updated', runs uv lock then unlocked uv sync under restored set -e, recomputes UV_LOCK_SHA256 and EXPECTED_INPUTS_HASH in the auto-heal arm before the sentinel write, removes SYNC_OUTPUT in success/auto-heal/non-drift arms, and exits with "$SYNC_RC" for non-drift failure.

sense_checks.question: Does the new test use `script.rindex(...)` (not `index`) for the auto-heal sentinel-write ordering check, assert all of {set +e/SYNC_RC=$?/set -e, drift grep, warning marker, both auto-heal commands without --locked, post-heal sha256sum recompute AFTER the warning, post-heal success line, non-drift abort line}, and leave the existing `test_rendered_startup_script_gates_uv_sync_on_inputs_sentinel` untouched and still passing?
sense_checks.executor_note: Yes. The new test uses script.rindex(...) for the auto-heal sentinel-write ordering check, asserts set +e/SYNC_RC=$?/set -e around the captured sync, the exact drift grep, warning marker, uv lock, unlocked recovery sync, post-warning sha256 recompute, auto-heal success line, and non-drift abort line. The existing sentinel-skip test was not modified and passed in the full test-file run.

sense_checks.question: Did `pytest tests/gpu_orchestrator/runpod/test_startup_script.py -x` and `pytest tests/gpu_orchestrator/ -x` both pass green, did the throwaway repro confirm the rendered script's auto-heal branch shape, and did the manual diff confirm `set -e` is restored before the auto-heal commands and `$SYNC_OUTPUT` is removed in all three arms?
sense_checks.executor_note: Yes. Both required pytest commands passed. The throwaway /tmp verifier confirmed the rendered auto-heal branch shape and was deleted. Manual rendered-block review confirmed set -e is restored before auto-heal uv lock/recovery sync, SYNC_OUTPUT cleanup appears in success/auto-heal/non-drift arms, and the success-arm sentinel write remains structurally first.

meta_commentary:
Two-file change: `gpu_orchestrator/runpod/worker_startup.template.sh` (the bash template, surgical edit to the else branch around lines 374–379) and `tests/gpu_orchestrator/runpod/test_startup_script.py` (one new test, leaving the existing sentinel-skip test untouched). The renderer `gpu_orchestrator/runpod/startup_script.py` loads the template verbatim — do not edit it.

Critical executor gotcha (FLAG-001): the success-arm `printf '%s\\n' \"$EXPECTED_INPUTS_HASH\" > \"$SYNC_SENTINEL\"` line and the auto-heal-arm sentinel-write line are byte-identical. The new test MUST use `script.rindex(...)` for the auto-heal ordering check, otherwise it will mis-locate to the success-arm position. Conversely, the EXISTING test relies on `script.index(...)` returning the success-arm (first) occurrence — so keep the success-arm sentinel write as the first such literal in document order.

`set -e` discipline: the only spot where you locally toggle `set +e` is around the captured `uv sync --locked` invocation. Re-enable `set -e` immediately. The auto-heal `uv lock` and recovery `uv sync` run under `set -e`, so a real lock-resolution failure (e.g. unsatisfiable constraints) aborts via the existing ERR trap — exactly what the brief requires for "If this fails, hard-fail the script."

Sentinel recomputation: the post-heal `EXPECTED_INPUTS_HASH` must use the same field order/recipe the template already uses earlier (uv-version, python: 3.10, extras: cuda124, uv-lock-sha256, pyproject-sha256). Read those lines (around 355–363) before writing the auto-heal recipe so the two hashes are interchangeable. `$PYPROJECT_SHA256` and `$UV_VERSION_STR` are already in scope from earlier in the script — reuse them rather than recomputing.

Marker scope is intentionally narrow. Do not broaden the drift regex to a generic `lockfile` substring — that risks classifying real resolution failures as drift and silently rebuilding a broken lock. The narrow `lockfile.*needs to be updated` (case-insensitive) is the only message we have production evidence for; if uv changes the wording in a future release, that's a deliberate re-evaluation moment, not a place to be defensively loose now.

`$SYNC_OUTPUT` cleanup: the gate's robustness pass made this explicit. All three arms (success, auto-heal, non-drift abort) must `rm -f "$SYNC_OUTPUT"`. Pod volumes can persist across reboots, so a leaked mktemp file would slowly accumulate.

Throwaway repro for T3: keep it lightweight. Render the template via the existing helper from the test file, grep the rendered string for the auto-heal substrings, and assert ordering — that's enough to confirm the structural change. Delete the script after running. Do NOT try to actually exercise `uv sync` on a real `uv.lock`; the template is bash, the test contract is structural.

Style match: bash-3 only (POSIX `[ ... ]`, no `[[ ]]`). The captured-output pattern should look like the rest of the template — single-quoted heredoc-friendly literals, `$VAR` not `${VAR}` unless disambiguation needs it, and no trailing whitespace.

plan_steps_covered.plan_step_summary: Replace the else branch with capture-and-branch logic that handles success, lockfile-drift auto-heal (with post-heal hash recomputation), and non-drift abort, all with $SYNC_OUTPUT cleanup and proper set -e/+e discipline

Add a new test asserting the auto-heal structure (drift grep, warning, both unlocked recovery commands, post-heal recompute, ordering via rindex, non-drift abort line) without disturbing the existing sentinel-skip test
Run targeted then broader pytest suites; do a manual visual diff of the rendered else block to confirm set -e restoration and $SYNC_OUTPUT cleanup in all three arms

validation.completeness_notes: All three plan steps mapped 1:1 to tasks. The throwaway repro requested by the finalize requirements is folded into T3's test-validation step (rendering the template and grep-asserting auto-heal substrings) since the bug is a structural property of the rendered bash, not a runtime behavior reproducible from Python.

baseline_test_note: Baseline tests not executed in this finalize step — environment is the planning harness, not the executor. The executor should run `pytest tests/gpu_orchestrator/runpod/test_startup_script.py -x` BEFORE making changes to confirm a clean baseline, then re-run after T1+T2 to confirm both the existing `test_rendered_startup_script_gates_uv_sync_on_inputs_sentinel` and the new `test_rendered_startup_script_auto_heals_lockfile_drift` pass.
